citation_short: SOIC case study on Second Sudanese Civil War (2025)
title: Second Sudanese Civil War 1983–2005
author_or_org: Study of Internal Conflict / US Army War College
strengths: Compact, citable overview of war dates, belligerents, and broad strategic arc.
limitations: Not a conflict-specific mortality or deployment reconstruction; not suitable for DR numerators or denominators.
notes: Used only for broad family framing and why the 1983-2005 war is separate from Darfur and the 2023-present SAF-RSF war.

citation_short: HRW Sudan, Oil, and Human Rights (2003)
title: Sudan, Oil, and Human Rights
strengths: Detailed treatment of oil-war displacement and why the post-1991 oil-belt theater needs its own denominator frame.
limitations: Does not provide a clean, bounded direct-death and force-exposure series for DR calculation.
notes: Used mainly to justify keeping post-Nasir oil-belt wars separate rather than rolling them into a whole-south denominator.

title: Darfur: Counting the Deaths
author_or_org: Centre for Research on the Epidemiology of Disasters (CRED)
geographic_scope: Darfur and eastern Chad refugee spillover
strengths: Still one of the clearest bounded mortality syntheses for the Darfur peak period, with explicit violent-versus-total mortality language and an affected-population series.
limitations: Violent deaths are not status-separated into civilian/combatant; combatant exposure remains weak; authors themselves note extrapolation limits.
notes: This source anchors the Darfur core window but is not enough on its own to publish a DR.

citation_short: GAO review of Darfur death estimates (2006)
title: Darfur Crisis: Death Estimates Demonstrate Severity of Crisis, but Their Accuracy and Credibility Could Be Enhanced
strengths: Explains why Darfur mortality estimates diverge and where extrapolation risk enters.
limitations: Not a new mortality estimate and not directly usable as a numerator or denominator source.
notes: Used to explain why a mechanistic average of Darfur death estimates would be misleading.

citation_short: OHCHR monitoring mission on West Darfur refugees in Chad (2023)
title: OHCHR Sudan report: Monitoring mission to Chad - June 2023
author_or_org: Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights
geographic_scope: El Geneina and road to Adré, Chad
strengths: Explicit corridor-level dating and strong attribution to RSF and/or allied Arab militias for killings of fleeing civilians.
limitations: Victim counts are partial and incident-based rather than a complete citywide rollup.
notes: Critical for including the Chad-corridor portion of the El Geneina episode.

citation_short: OHCHR mass grave statement on El Geneina (2023)
title: Sudan: At least 87 buried in mass grave in Darfur as Rapid Support Forces deny victims decent burials
author_or_org: Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights
strengths: Specific, attributable incident count with women and children among the dead.
limitations: Only one grave-site event, not a full-episode total.
notes: Useful as a hard lower-bound incident and subgroup datapoint.

title: The Massalit Will Not Come Home: Ethnic Cleansing and Crimes Against Humanity in El Geneina, West Darfur, Sudan
strengths: Most detailed single reconstruction of El Geneina atrocities and local force structure; useful for both numerators and denominator framing.
limitations: Whole-year estimates are broader than the April-June window; combatant fatalities are not directly quantified.
notes: Core source for episode boundaries and force-mobilization notes.

citation_short: Reuters investigation on El Geneina massacre (2023)
title: How Arab fighters carried out a rolling ethnic massacre in Sudan
strengths: Strong named-source reporting, satellite corroboration, and useful city-specific displacement/death figures.
limitations: Still not a full registry; some numbers are witness-based or monitor-based rather than official tallies.
notes: Key bridge between lower-bound burial evidence and higher monitor-based totals.

citation_short: BMJ/MSF mortality surveys among West Darfur refugees (2025)
title: High mortality and violence among refugees and returnees from West Darfur, Sudan arriving in Chad, 2023: results from three retrospective mortality surveys
author_or_org: BMJ Global Health / Médecins Sans Frontières-linked authors
geographic_scope: Refugee camps in Chad, with household origins in El Geneina and other West Darfur towns
strengths: Peer-reviewed, quantitative, and especially useful for severity/plausibility checks and subgroup patterning.
limitations: Camp-origin households are not a full city census; selection and survival biases mean it should not be treated as a direct city total.
notes: Used as a plausibility check, not as the main El Geneina citywide total.

title: West Darfur pre-crisis profile / thematic report
date_scope: baseline 2022 with conflict update to mid-2023
strengths: Useful denominator context and humanitarian baselines, especially for West Darfur and pre-existing displacement.
limitations: State-level, not city-specific; mixes multiple secondary sources.
notes: Used as context only, not as the primary El Geneina city denominator.

title: Sudan: Killings in Ardamata
author_or_org: Office of the UN High Commissioner for Human Rights
strengths: Explicitly identifies RSF/allied Arab militia as perpetrators and Massalit civilians as primary victims after the base takeover.
limitations: Does not provide a full final death tally by status.
notes: Key attribution source for the Ardamata massacre window.

title: Sudan: New Mass Ethnic Killings, Pillage in Darfur
pages_or_sections: main text, especially early estimate paragraphs and findings sections
strengths: Good triangulation of death toll range, civilian targeting, and displacement; also captures detentions and shelling complexity.
limitations: Death toll range partly relies on linked secondary reporting; still incomplete on combatant fatalities.
notes: Best single short-form source for the civilian death range in Ardamata.

title: Sudan refugees detail second wave of bloody ethnic purge by Arab forces
strengths: Strong survivor/soldier testimony, explicit note that tens of thousands were present, and unusually useful defender-strength clues.
limitations: Still lacks a complete status-separated fatality registry and can only partly constrain military deaths.
notes: Crucial for the Ardamata denominator frame.

title: Ethnically Motivated Attacks in Ardamata, West Darfur
author_or_org: African Centre for Justice and Peace Studies
strengths: Useful on local combatant composition, the 15th Division base, and mixed civilian/soldier losses.
limitations: Not a gold-standard casualty registry; some figures are from 'reliable sources' rather than fully transparent microdata.
notes: Used cautiously to bracket military and unknown-status components, not as the sole source.

citation_short: Lancet Global Health Khartoum mortality study (2025)
title: War-time mortality in Sudan: a multiple systems estimation analysis
author_or_org: Maysoon Dahab et al. / Lancet Global Health
strengths: Best available empirical mortality study for Khartoum State, with explicit all-cause and intentional-injury totals.
limitations: Intentional injuries are not cleanly separated into civilian vs combatant deaths; does not solve DR denominators.
notes: Used to justify keeping Khartoum as a proposed episode despite withholding a DR.

citation_short: Reuters on El Fasher siege toll (2024)
title: More than 700 killed in siege of Sudan's al-Fashir, UN rights chief says
strengths: Current siege-specific civilian death/injury count with explicit warning that both RSF shelling and SAF airstrikes contributed.
limitations: Ongoing siege; not status-separated beyond civilian casualties; no stable denominator.
notes: Used only for proposed-episode justification and open problems.
